title: Tithymalus calyptratus

This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. The genus Tithymalus is a group of plants that are often placed within the broader classification of Euphorbia, although some sources may recognize Tithymalus as a distinct genus. The classification of Tithymalus calyptratus continues to be refined as new research and data become available.

intro: Tithymalus calyptratus, also known as Euphorbia calyptrata, is a plant species within the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae) characterized by its unique, capitate inflorescences. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including inflammation, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its numerous health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties.
